description:
Executor the desktop workflow application for Windows. Speed up your daily workflow, opening things and doing things.

Executor the desktop workflow application for Windows. Speed up your daily workflow, opening things and doing things.

Key Features:
- Launch applications, files, folders, websites, web services (with parameters) and prompts in milliseconds.
- Trigger multiple actions with one text command and/or hotkey.
- Let's be an expert in opening up the world instead of trying to put the whole world inside of Executor (as some alternatives try to do).
- Take the clipboard to a new level, swiftly trigger system commands, do fast calculations, manage running applications and more.
- Tailor keywords, appearance, hotkeys, and behavior to perfectly match your daily workflow.
- Customize the look and feel with various skins and theming options to match your desktop setup.
Improvements
- $SENDTEXT...$ keyword commands now support using a text file for the text to send. This is done by in the keyword parameter setting the tag {FILE c:\some folder\mytext.txt}. This can be combined with other SENDTEXT tags.
- Recently the ability to tag keywords with url was added. Where when your current input is a URL, the Input Assist automatically suggests these tagged keywords to open it. This functionality now also automatically works for any keywords that use a URL directly as their command.
  How to use:
  - Type your input (e.g., g free football streaming).
  - Cycle Modes: Press Ctrl+Space a few times until you see the type:url(keyword) content mode.
  - See Suggestions: Input Assist will now display all your URL-handling keywords (that you tagged with url). This is handy if you want to quickly route a link or search query to different browsers or specific browser profiles (e.g., Home, Work).
  Note: If your input includes a parameter (like the search term in the example above), it will automatically be passed along to the selected keyword. Also if the input keyword command uses $DEF$ this will be respected as well.
